name: JBSA Fort Sam Houston
tagline: The home of military medicine: Brooke Army Medical Center and the military's medical training hub.
_note: JBSA base (San Antonio). Verified from official sources (jbsa.mil, home.army.mil/samhouston, bamc.tricare.mil, METC/MEDCoE, fshisd.net, DeCA/AAFES, DoD BAH tool). MHA TX285 (same as the other JBSA bases). Medium/low-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 78209, surrounding off-post school districts (do not publish specific assignment).

data.Also hosts: METC + Army Medical Center of Excellence (MEDCoE)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: JBSA-Fort Sam Houston is the center of U.S. military medicine. Its signature tenant is Brooke Army Medical Center (BAMC), the Army's flagship medical institution and the Defense Department's only Level I Trauma Center.

The post also hosts the Medical Education & Training Campus (METC), which trains enlisted medics for the Army, Navy, Air Force, and Coast Guard, plus the U.S. Army Medical Center of Excellence (MEDCoE). Installation support is provided by the Air Force's 502d Air Base Wing.

data.subtitle: Fort Sam Houston is the center of military medicine, which makes it one of the best-resourced bases in the country for a family's healthcare.
intro: Brooke Army Medical Center anchors the entire San Antonio military health system. It's the DoD's flagship medical center and its only Level I Trauma Center, which means deep healthcare access for families on and around the post.

For many service members, an assignment here is tied to medical training or care, given METC and MEDCoE. It has a different feel from an operational post, more campus and care than flight line or motor pool.

recommendations.advice: BAMC serves the whole San Antonio military community; confirm enrollment and referrals through TRICARE West.
Established neighborhoods like Alamo Heights / Terrell Hills sit just off post, with a short commute to central San Antonio.
You're minutes from downtown: the River Walk, the Pearl, and the rest of San Antonio.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official JBSA-Fort Sam Houston visitor information page before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: On-post family housing is privatized, managed by Hunt Military Communities (which took over management in 2021).
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so check the garrison housing office and the partner for current options and waitlists.

source.label: DoD BAH Rate Lookup (MHA TX285, CY2026)

sections.title: Schools
data: On post, schooling is the Fort Sam Houston Independent School District (FSHISD), a Texas public district physically on the installation (Fort Sam Houston Elementary; Robert G. Cole Middle/High). It is not a DoDEA district.
Off post, school assignment depends on your San Antonio address, so confirm the specific district and campus for any neighborhood you're considering.

data.Distinction: DoD's only Level I Trauma Center

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the answer is mostly about how long you'll be here and how steady the local market is.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the area before committing to a neighborhood.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The market is steady and you could rent the home out later if needed.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length and the local market, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on off-base neighborhoods before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining school district or DoDEA early and bring records and immunizations. The School Liaison Office can help with the transfer.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE region and enrollment so you can be seen at the local military treatment facility or network providers.

data.q: What makes Fort Sam Houston unique?
data.a: It is the center of military medicine, home to Brooke Army Medical Center (the DoD's only Level I Trauma Center) and the medical training campuses, METC and MEDCoE.
Established neighborhoods like Alamo Heights and Terrell Hills sit just off post, with quick access to central San Antonio. On-post housing is run by Hunt Military Communities.
Yes. BAMC anchors the San Antonio military health system, so healthcare access is among the deepest of any base. Confirm enrollment through TRICARE West.
MHA TX285 (San Antonio), shared with the other JBSA bases. Use the calculator above.

sections.title: Food & Things Nearby
sections.note: Live nearby places via Google Places. Tap 'Use my location' to re-center on where you are.

picks.name: The Alamo
picks.blurb: The iconic 1718 mission and battle site in the heart of downtown San Antonio. Free to enter.

picks.name: San Antonio River Walk
picks.blurb: The city's signature network of riverside walkways, restaurants, and boat tours, just below street level downtown.

picks.name: The Pearl
picks.blurb: A former brewery turned dining and shopping district north of downtown, with a popular weekend farmers market.

picks.name: San Antonio Missions National Historical Park
picks.blurb: Four Spanish colonial missions preserved by the National Park Service along the river, part of Texas's only UNESCO World Heritage Site.
picks.maps_query: San Antonio Missions National Historical Park
